claim: Retrieval is strong (TF-IDF R@1 ~0.91 orig; ~0.90 noisy)
evidence_source: Local rerun this session (331-query eval files + compute_metrics)
notes: Orig TF-IDF 0.9094 (vs manuscript 0.9063); noisy TF-IDF 0.9033 (exact match). BM25 orig 0.8822 vs 0.8852 (-0.003). All deltas within 1 query and explained by 4-entry catalog expansion.

claim: Downstream grounding is the main bottleneck
evidence_source: Manuscript docs (code paths unchanged, verified by grep)
notes: InstantiationReady ≤0.085 even with oracle schema. Oracle covers 0.870 vs TF-IDF 0.822 (+0.048). Retrieval not the only limiter; extraction/assignment bottleneck confirmed structurally.

claim: Deterministic structured repair improves over typed greedy on at least one metric
notes: Semantic IR repair TypeMatch 0.254 vs typed 0.227 (+0.027). Opt-role repair Exact20 0.277 vs typed 0.214 (+0.063). Coverage preserved for opt-role. Constrained has best Exact20 (0.328) but lowest InstReady (0.027).

claim: Acceptance-style methods improve InstantiationReady vs typed greedy
notes: tfidf_accept_rerank: InstReady 0.0816 vs typed 0.0725 (+0.009). Hierarchical: 0.0846 (+0.012). Trade-off: Schema R@1 drops from 0.906 to 0.876/0.846.

claim: Float grounding remains a major weakness
notes: float TypeMatch ≈0.029; integer TypeMatch ≈0.991. Float params are the largest group (n_expected=1209 vs integer 147). Low type_match for floats is the dominant quality gap.

claim: Learning has not produced trustworthy gains
notes: All 5 learned runs used rule-baseline fallback (no torch installed). Metrics identical across all runs. No checkpoint produced. Frames paper as deterministic-only contribution.

claim: Branch hybrid BM25+TF-IDF retriever improves short-query R@1
notes: Short R@1: hybrid 0.7915 vs TF-IDF 0.7795 (+0.012). Orig: 0.9094 (same). Noisy: 0.9003 vs TF-IDF 0.9033 (-0.003, within noise). Net positive.

claim: Branch slot-aware extraction (SAE) achieves 100% value-exact on hand-crafted cases
notes: 24/24 = 100% exact match on 4 hand-crafted LP cases (product-mix, diet, investment, transportation). Previous global_consistency_grounding achieved ~20% on same cases.

claim: SAE fills 62.9% of schema slots on NLP4LP orig queries
notes: Mean 4.6/7.4 slots filled per query (62.9%). 7/330 queries have 0 fills (no numeric tokens found). Coverage comparable to full downstream pipeline (0.822) but without gold data.

claim: Catalog expansion (4 open-domain entries) does not hurt NLP4LP retrieval
notes: Orig TF-IDF: 335-entry catalog 0.9094 vs 331-entry 0.9063 (+0.003). Short: 335-entry 0.7795 vs 331-entry 0.7825 (-0.003). Minimal impact.

claim: Manuscript can be framed as deterministic/expert-system paper now
notes: All methods are deterministic. Strong retrieval story. Downstream bottleneck clearly identified. Multiple ablations. No LLM dependency. Interpretable trade-offs publishable as findings.

claim: Full downstream fresh rerun with gold parameter values
evidence_source: N/A — HF gated dataset udell-lab/NLP4LP offline
notes: Downstream numbers from docs are code-path-verified (unchanged pipeline). New SAE method not tested on full 331-query pipeline with gold labels. Would strengthen paper but not required for submission.

evidence_source: N/A — GPU/Wulver required; torch not installed
notes: Paper is deterministic-only. Learning is future work or negative result. Not needed for current submission framing.
Single benchmark (NLP4LP 331). Reviewer concern is predictable. Should be stated as limitation. Not fatal for workshop/conference paper.
InstantiationReady is threshold-based (coverage≥0.8 AND type_match≥0.8). Not solver-verified. Should be stated as caveat. Does not block submission.

claim: Noisy variant downstream metrics are meaningful (non-zero)
notes: Noisy uses <num> placeholders; TypeMatch=0 and InstReady=0 by design. Retrieval R@1=0.903 still strong. Must include caveat in paper: noisy downstream = retrieval-only narrative.
Retrieval table: random=1/331 (theoretical). Downstream: random=2/331 (empirical, one run). Should align to one definition or clearly label both. Documentation fix only, no re-run needed.
